title: Neocheiropteris maculosa
intro: Neocheiropteris maculosa, commonly known as the spotted tree fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Neocheiropteris maculosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Neocheiropteris maculosa belongs to the family Polypodiaceae and is a species of fern. It is classified under the genus Neocheiropteris and is a type of terrestrial fern found in Southeast Asia. The plant is also known by its synonym, Cheiroglossa maculosa. Its classification at the rank of order is Polypodiales, and at the rank of division is Pteridophyta.
uses: Neocheiropteris maculosa has been found to have potential medicinal properties, but more research is needed to confirm its applications. In some studies, extracts from Neocheiropteris maculosa have shown promise in treating certain health conditions, such as wound healing and skin infections.
